name: PlantFit Ralphthon MVP
description: Sequential single-runner execution plan for a Ralphthon-ready PlantFit web MVP with Hogangnono XPath automation, cinematic sunlight reveals, responsive placement interactions, and evidence-driven Playwright evaluation loops.
executionNotes: Run exactly one active implementation story at a time on one machine.
Do not parallelize coding, review, or browser evaluation tasks.
Every UI-affecting story must be judged with Playwright screenshots before it can pass.
If an evaluation fails, fix the issue, rerun the evaluation, and keep looping until all pass criteria are satisfied.

loopProtocol: Implement the story and its subtasks.
Run the story evaluation set in order.
Capture Playwright screenshots for every required viewport and state.
Judge the result against the wireframe and visual direction docs.
If any check fails, record the reason, fix the issue, and rerun the full failing evaluation.
Mark the story passed only when all evaluations pass and no critical visual or flow regressions remain.
hardFailConditions: Hero animation makes content harder to understand
Playwright interaction cannot complete the intended flow
Implemented screen looks materially different from the wireframe without a strong product reason

userStories.title: Create application shell and cinematic design system
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to feel like a striking, premium product so that the first screen already signals Ralphthon-level craft.
userStories.goal: Establish the reusable visual and structural foundation for all later screens, with strong data-cinematic identity and responsive behavior.
details: Build the base app shell, routing skeleton, and global layout primitives.
Define design tokens for color, spacing, glow, depth, glass surfaces, gradients, and motion timing.
Create shared UI primitives that later stories can reuse without visual drift.
Make the design system intentionally non-generic so the app does not resemble a default SaaS dashboard.
deliverables: Shared button, card, panel, tag, and section components
Base navigation and screen container patterns
acceptanceCriteria: Shared shell exists and can host every major screen defined in the wireframe
Visual tokens support glow, glass, depth, and atmospheric gradients
Desktop placement layout can coexist with mobile-first screens
The overall styling direction clearly reflects the approved data-cinematic brief

subtasks.title: Set up routing and root providers
details: Create the root app entry and route structure for all planned screens.
Add shared providers for session, theme, motion preferences, and seeded data access.
doneWhen: All required top-level routes exist
App renders without runtime boot errors

subtasks.title: Define visual tokens and theme primitives
details: Create semantic tokens for sunlight gold, healthy green, moderate lime, cool shadow, neutral text, glass surfaces, and elevated shadows.
Add motion tokens for reveal speed, stagger timing, glow intensity, and scan sweep duration.
doneWhen: Tokens are reusable
No hardcoded ad hoc colors dominate core screens

subtasks.title: Create shared cinematic UI primitives
details: Build reusable button, card, status chip, panel, navbar, and bottom-action primitives.
Ensure primitives support the spatial depth and glass-like surface treatment approved for Ralphthon.
doneWhen: Primitives are reusable
Panels and buttons look deliberate rather than default

subtasks.title: Create responsive shell patterns
details: Support mobile-first screen containers and a wider desktop surface for the placement board.
Prevent overflow, clipping, or dead zones on narrow devices.
doneWhen: Major shell components adapt to mobile and desktop
No obvious overflow occurs

subtasks.title: Add motion safety and reduced-effect guardrails
details: Implement a reduced-motion path or effect simplification mechanism.
Ensure effects can degrade gracefully on weaker devices without destroying hierarchy.
doneWhen: Core UI remains usable with reduced motion
Animations do not hard-block interaction

evaluationSet.name: Command quality gates
passCriteria: Both commands exit successfully
No new warnings or build-time blockers remain unexplained
failActions: Do not continue to visual review until both commands pass

evaluationSet.name: Root shell mobile visual review
actions: Inspect header, hero, cards, and primary CTA
passCriteria: The shell looks premium and intentional, not like default boilerplate
Primary CTA is visually dominant
Spacing and typography hierarchy are clean
No clipped or overlapping content is visible
reviewRubric: Visual fidelity to the wireframe is at least 4/5
Interaction clarity is at least 4/5
Readability and hierarchy are at least 4/5
Layout stability is 5/5
Wow factor is at least 4/5
failActions: Write down the exact visual or interaction mismatch
Rejudge from scratch rather than assuming nearby changes are acceptable

evaluationSet.name: Placement shell desktop visual review
setup: If the route requires stub data, inject minimal safe fixture state before navigation
actions: Open the placement route even if content is stubbed
Inspect desktop board framing, panels, and space allocation
passCriteria: Desktop layout clearly reserves a main board surface and supporting side or bottom panel areas
The shell already suggests a high-value interactive product surface
The route does not look broken or unfinished
reviewRubric: Visual fidelity to the wireframe is at least 4/5
Interaction clarity is at least 4/5
Readability and hierarchy are at least 4/5
Layout stability is 5/5
Wow factor is at least 4/5
failActions: Write down the exact visual or interaction mismatch

mustCheck: Visual identity is distinct and memorable
The design language can support all later hero moments
No generic template feel remains in the base shell

stopOnlyWhen: No critical visual or flow issue remains open

userStories.title: Define domain model, persistence, and fallback fixture data
userStories.description: As the system, I want stable local data contracts so that every flow can function even when live automation is unavailable.
userStories.goal: Create the typed data backbone, browser persistence layer, and seeded analysis packets that make the sequential demo reliable.
details: Formalize all main data types needed across the analysis, recommendation, placement, and care flows.
Provide fallback data that keeps the product demo alive even if automation fails or is skipped.
Persist user state so the experience survives refreshes and route changes.
acceptanceCriteria: All screen routes can render from either live automation output or fallback fixture data
Session, placements, registered plants, and care logs persist across refresh
Guard helpers prevent route crashes when expected prior state is missing

subtasks.title: Define analysis and automation result types
details: Create type contracts for orientation evidence, floorplan artifacts, sunlight captures, normalized light zones, and final analysis packets.
doneWhen: Types cover all major flows
No ambiguous any-like contracts remain in core paths

subtasks.title: Define plant, placement, and care data contracts
details: Create contracts for recommendations, placed plants, registered plants, reminder states, and care logs.
doneWhen: Placement and care screens can read from the contracts without ad hoc transformation

subtasks.title: Create seeded fallback analysis packets
details: Seed at least one address-derived analysis result with realistic floorplan zones, room labels, and seasonal summaries.
Ensure fallback data is product-grade rather than obviously fake placeholders.
doneWhen: Result, recommendation, and placement screens can render fully from fallback data

subtasks.title: Create seeded plant catalog and mapping helpers
details: Seed enough plants to support recommendation diversity across light strengths and beginner difficulty filters.
doneWhen: Recommendation screen can show 3-5 coherent options per light context

subtasks.title: Implement browser storage adapters
details: Persist analysis session, selected plants, placements, registered plants, and care logs.
Add safe parse, versioning, and reset helpers where useful.
doneWhen: Refresh preserves state
Corrupt or missing storage does not crash the app

subtasks.title: Implement route guard and fallback loaders
details: If a later route lacks required state, redirect or inject a safe fallback path rather than crashing.
doneWhen: Direct entry to later routes results in a recoverable experience

evaluationSet.name: Command quality gates
passCriteria: Both commands exit successfully
No new warnings or build-time blockers remain unexplained
failActions: Do not continue to visual review until both commands pass

evaluationSet.name: Data contract integrity review
steps: Inspect the main data types and confirm they cover automation, results, recommendations, placements, and care.
Inspect seeded fixtures and confirm they are sufficient to render all downstream UI.
passCriteria: No core screen requires undefined ad hoc shape invention
Fallback analysis data contains enough information for result, recommendation, and placement views
failActions: Update fixtures and retry the review

evaluationSet.name: Persistence and reload smoke review
setup: Use page.evaluate to seed storage if no UI-based path exists yet
actions: Preload fallback analysis session into browser storage if needed
Confirm the same state is still visible
passCriteria: The result route renders from fallback or stored data without crashing
Refresh preserves the visible state
No broken empty state appears after refresh
reviewRubric: Visual fidelity to the wireframe is at least 4/5
Interaction clarity is at least 4/5
Readability and hierarchy are at least 4/5
Layout stability is 5/5
Overall craft quality is at least 4/5
failActions: Write down the exact visual or interaction mismatch

userStories.title: Build Hogangnono automation pipeline and evidence contract
userStories.description: As the system, I want a real address automation path so that PlantFit can acquire orientation, floorplan, and sunlight evidence from Hogangnono.
userStories.goal: Turn the validated XPath reference implementation into the primary address-based acquisition path for the MVP, with structured artifacts and graceful failure behavior.
details: Use the validated XPath approach from ~/workspace/ttt as the implementation reference.
Keep selectors inspectable and aligned with the PRD-defined flows.
Preserve evidence, screenshots, and logs because selector maintenance is part of the real product risk.
Never let automation failure hard-stop the entire product journey.
acceptanceCriteria: The address path can trigger Playwright automation against Hogangnono
The automation returns structured status, extracted values, screenshots, and logs
Collector failures remain inspectable and do not dead-end the app

subtasks.title: Port or recreate the shared Hogangnono navigation helper
details: Implement the base function that opens Hogangnono, searches an address, and lands on the apartment page.
doneWhen: Address search can consistently land on the target apartment page under nominal conditions

subtasks.title: Implement orientation collector
details: Use the PRD XPath list first.
Capture raw SVG path evidence and compute orientation when possible.
Save screenshot evidence.
doneWhen: Collector returns elementFound, raw fields, computedAngle, screenshots, and error state

subtasks.title: Implement floorplan collector
details: Collect available unit options, selected unit, and floorplan image when present.
Download or preserve the image as an artifact and create human-reviewable evidence.
doneWhen: Collector returns image URL or clear failure with evidence

subtasks.title: Implement sunlight collector
details: Enter the sunlight section, attempt to open the 3D sunlight viewer, and capture at least one evidence screenshot.
doneWhen: Collector returns viewerOpened, canvasDetected, captures, screenshots, and error state

subtasks.title: Implement runner orchestration and unified result contract
details: Run collectors sequentially, accumulate logs, compute overall status, and return one stable result object.
doneWhen: One runner call can produce a normalized bundle for the UI

subtasks.title: Implement graceful fallback bridge
details: If the automation result is partial or failed, map to fallback analysis packets so the product flow continues.
doneWhen: Automation failure no longer blocks downstream screens

evaluationSet.name: Command quality gates
passCriteria: Both commands exit successfully
No new warnings or build-time blockers remain unexplained
failActions: Do not continue to visual review until both commands pass

evaluationSet.name: Automation contract review
steps: Inspect the automation runner output shape.
Confirm orientation, floorplan, sunlight, runMeta, input, and logs are all present.
passCriteria: The contract is stable and explicit
Each collector can fail without collapsing the full result object

evaluationSet.name: Automation console or flow smoke review
actions: Wait for automation status to update
Inspect surfaced evidence or progress state
passCriteria: The UI clearly indicates that automation is running or has run
A successful or partial evidence payload can be surfaced to the app layer
Failure messaging is structured and understandable

Interaction clarity is at least 4/5
Readability and hierarchy are at least 4/5
Layout stability is 5/5
Overall craft quality is at least 4/5
failActions: Write down the exact visual or interaction mismatch

userStories.title: Build home screen, method selection, and navigation guards
userStories.description: As a first-time user, I want a frictionless entry flow so that I immediately understand the product and can start the right analysis path.
userStories.goal: Implement the opening experience and the two-path decision point while protecting the flow from broken state transitions.
details: The first screens must be beautiful and clear, but still secondary to the later hero moments.
The user should never feel trapped or confused about how to start the product.
acceptanceCriteria: Home screen mirrors the wireframe in structure and messaging
Method selection clearly offers photo and address paths
Existing analysis state changes what the home screen shows
No dead-end or broken back-navigation exists in the early flow

subtasks.title: Implement first-launch hero and CTA region
details: Add the main headline, supporting copy, and primary CTA as defined by the wireframe.
doneWhen: Home route presents the value proposition instantly

subtasks.title: Implement empty-state result area
details: Show a guided empty state before analysis exists and a preview slot once analysis data exists.
deliverables: Home empty state and result preview state
doneWhen: Home route changes meaningfully based on saved analysis state

subtasks.title: Implement analysis method selection screen
details: Create the two-card decision screen for photo upload and address input.
doneWhen: Both entry paths are obvious and tappable

subtasks.title: Implement route transitions and guardrails
details: Support forward and backward transitions and prevent invalid entry to later screens.
doneWhen: User cannot get stuck or land on impossible intermediate states

subtasks.title: Polish early-flow motion
details: Add restrained transitions so the opening flow feels premium without overshadowing the later hero moments.
doneWhen: Early flow feels crafted and responsive

userStories.title: Build mocked photo analysis flow with direction confirmation
userStories.description: As a user, I want a believable photo-based path so that I can imagine the product understanding my room from photos.
userStories.goal: Implement the wireframe-complete photo path even though the intelligence layer is simulated in v1.
details: The UI must feel plausible and product-grade even though image understanding is mocked.
Direction confirmation and fallback must be clear and easy to use on mobile.
acceptanceCriteria: User can upload 1 to 3 photos
User sees a mock AI inference result
User can reject the inference and choose a manual direction
The path stores enough state to continue to loading

subtasks.title: Implement photo upload slots and constraints
details: Create UI for selecting, previewing, and limiting uploads to 3 photos.
doneWhen: Selecting and previewing 1-3 images works on mobile

subtasks.title: Implement photo metadata labeling
details: Allow each uploaded image to carry a simple contextual label such as living room or bedroom.
doneWhen: Uploaded photos are distinguishable in the UI

subtasks.title: Implement mocked AI direction result state
details: After upload, transition to a believable AI confirmation state for inferred direction.
doneWhen: The result feels credible and transitions cleanly

subtasks.title: Implement manual direction fallback
details: If the user rejects the suggestion, show text-based direction choices.
doneWhen: User can complete the flow without accepting the mock inference

subtasks.title: Persist photo-flow analysis input state
details: Save uploaded-photo metadata and confirmed direction choices into the session store.
doneWhen: The loading route can consume the saved photo-input payload

userStories.title: Build address analysis flow with automation-backed confirmation and fallback
userStories.description: As a user, I want the address route to feel real and reliable so that I trust the app is genuinely reading my space.
userStories.goal: Connect the user-facing address flow to the Hogangnono automation path, while preserving a graceful fallback if automation is partial or unavailable.
details: This is the primary real acquisition flow for v1.
The user should feel that the system is doing actual work, even when it later degrades into fallback data.
deliverables: Collected evidence summary or derived building info state
acceptanceCriteria: User can enter an address and trigger automation
Automation outcome is reflected in the UI
User can continue even if the automation result is partial or failed
The route never dead-ends

subtasks.title: Implement address input surface
details: Create a focused address input screen with clear prompt and primary submit action.
doneWhen: User can enter and submit a target address from mobile and desktop

subtasks.title: Connect UI to the automation runner
details: Trigger the automation pipeline and display live progress or status transitions.
doneWhen: Submitting an address starts the automation path

subtasks.title: Render confirmation state from automation output
details: Surface address, orientation clues, building information, or collected evidence summary in a confirmation layout.
doneWhen: User sees a coherent confirmation state before proceeding

subtasks.title: Implement fallback path when automation is partial or failed
details: Use seeded fallback packets to keep the flow alive if collectors fail or return incomplete data.
doneWhen: The user can still proceed to analysis loading when automation fails

subtasks.title: Persist the selected analysis packet
details: Store the chosen or derived address analysis packet for later loading and results.
doneWhen: Later screens can render from the chosen packet without requerying immediately

userStories.title: Build cinematic analysis loading sequence
userStories.description: As a user, I want the analysis phase to feel like a scan of my home so that I feel anticipation and trust before seeing the result.
userStories.goal: Create the first major Ralphthon wow moment: a loading screen that reveals progress through meaningful light-driven choreography.
details: This must not be a spinner with text.
The loading sequence should sell the fantasy that the home is being read, parsed, and illuminated.
acceptanceCriteria: The loading screen shows multiple intelligible progress stages
A directional light-scan or equivalent reveal is visible
The sequence transitions cleanly into the result screen
The effect is cinematic without obscuring the information

subtasks.title: Define loading stage model
details: Create at least four stages such as collecting signals, parsing layout, estimating light paths, and matching plants.
doneWhen: Loading states are ordered and understandable

subtasks.title: Create wireframe-like structural backdrop
details: Render a subtle background structure or floorplan outline behind the loading sequence.
doneWhen: The loading screen already hints at the spatial result to come

subtasks.title: Implement directional light-scan animation
details: Sweep a beam or mask across the structure to imply scanning and discovery.
doneWhen: The effect runs smoothly and explains progress rather than distracting from it

subtasks.title: Synchronize status copy and motion
details: Tie stage labels to the scan progression and reveal timing.
doneWhen: Loading copy and motion feel coherent

subtasks.title: Implement timed handoff into result route
details: Transition from loading into the result screen without a jarring cut.
doneWhen: The reveal feels like one continuous moment

userStories.title: Build sunlight result reveal and floorplan overlay experience
userStories.description: As a user, I want the result screen to make the invisible visible so that I immediately understand how light moves through my home.
userStories.goal: Create the second hero moment: a staged reveal of sunlight zones over the 2D floorplan with readable supporting data.
details: The result must arrive with a reveal, not an instant static dump.
The floorplan is the core surface; supporting cards and charts exist to reinforce the main board, not compete with it.
acceptanceCriteria: Light zones are visible, understandable, and tied to the floorplan
Seasonal summary is present and legible
The reveal looks cinematic while keeping text readable
The recommendation CTA is visually obvious

doneWhen: Board, cards, and CTA are composed in a readable hierarchy

subtasks.title: Implement floorplan overlay renderer
details: Render the 2D floorplan and overlay light zones with semantic colors.
doneWhen: Floorplan and zones align and remain readable

subtasks.title: Implement staged reveal animation
details: Reveal zone layers progressively to imply sunlight settling into the space.
doneWhen: The result appears dramatically without sacrificing clarity

subtasks.title: Implement room or zone summaries and seasonal support panel
details: Add concise supporting summaries that explain the board rather than distract from it.
doneWhen: User can understand strong, medium, and weak zones quickly

subtasks.title: Add CTA and share affordance
details: Provide the transition to recommendations and a placeholder share action matching the wireframe.
doneWhen: CTA is obvious and the result screen feels complete

userStories.title: Build recommendation list with beginner filter and selection state
userStories.description: As a user, I want recommendation cards that feel tailored and useful so that I can confidently move toward placement and registration.
userStories.goal: Translate the analysis result into a focused recommendation screen with enough product character to feel curated, not generic.

Recommendation cards must communicate fit, difficulty, and effect at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Screen shows 3-5 coherent plant recommendations
Beginner filter works and defaults on
Selection updates later placement or registration flows
Cards feel editorial rather than list-like

userStories.title: Build cinematic placement board with positive and negative zone feedback
userStories.description: As a user, I want the placement board to react to my choices so that placing a plant feels like interacting with a living spatial system.
userStories.goal: Create the third and strongest hero moment: a desktop-priority interactive board where plant placement visibly succeeds or fails based on light suitability.
details: This is the single highest-value interactive surface in the demo.
The board must feel alive, spatial, and immediately understandable.
If time or performance tradeoffs arise, preserve the core board reaction loop over peripheral polish.
deliverables: Positive and negative zone response effects
acceptanceCriteria: User can place and remove plants on the board
Good placements trigger visibly positive feedback
Bad placements trigger visibly negative feedback
Board remains understandable and stable on desktop
The surface reads as a Ralphthon hero screen

subtasks.title: Create desktop-first board layout
details: Allocate main board area, support panel, and selected item detail region in a clean desktop composition.
doneWhen: The board owns the screen and support panels do not crowd it

subtasks.title: Implement board interaction model
details: Support click-to-place or drag-and-drop in a way that is reliable on demo hardware.
doneWhen: User can place and remove plants predictably

subtasks.title: Map placements to zone evaluation
details: Determine whether a placement lands in a strong, moderate, or weak zone and attach a suitability verdict.
doneWhen: Each placement returns an interpretable suitability state

subtasks.title: Implement positive placement response
details: Add glow, aura, vitality, or other cinematic reinforcement for good placements.
doneWhen: Good placements feel rewarded immediately

subtasks.title: Implement negative placement response
details: Add warning wash, dimming, drained light, or other rejection cues for bad placements.
doneWhen: Bad placements feel visibly wrong immediately

subtasks.title: Implement placed-list management and guidance copy
details: Keep a clear list of placed plants and show textual advice tied to the current placement or selection.
doneWhen: The board has both visceral reaction and practical explanation

userStories.title: Build plant registration flow and character-driven care alerts
userStories.description: As a user, I want my chosen plant to become a named character so that the product shifts from analysis tool to emotional companion.
userStories.goal: Implement the bridge from recommendation and placement into ongoing care through naming, registration, and expressive reminder states.
details: The emotional layer should complement the data-cinematic system rather than hijack it into a purely cute product.
Care alerts should be expressive but still readable and product-safe.
acceptanceCriteria: Selected plants can be named and registered
Registration creates a persistent plant record
Care alerts show at least three expressive states
Users can act from care alerts back into relevant flows

subtasks.title: Build plant registration screen
details: Show selected plant context, intended placement context, and naming input.
doneWhen: User can understand what they are registering and where it belongs

subtasks.title: Persist registered plant records
details: Create and save records that include plant identity, nickname, placement reference, and care state.
doneWhen: Registered plant survives refresh and appears in care views

subtasks.title: Implement character presentation layer
details: Add mood states, expression treatment, or character cues without overloading the visual language.
doneWhen: Registered plant feels alive but still aligned with the product tone

subtasks.title: Implement care alert states
details: Create thirsty, neglected, and seasonal-move states with expressive copy.
doneWhen: At least three distinct care scenarios are represented

subtasks.title: Link care alerts to relevant actions
details: Allow users to acknowledge, care, or jump back to the placement board when appropriate.
doneWhen: Alerts lead to useful next actions rather than dead text

evaluationSet.name: Command quality gates
passCriteria: Both commands exit successfully
No new warnings or build-time blockers remain unexplained
failActions: Do not continue to visual review until both commands pass

actions: Seed a selected plant and placement context
passCriteria: The screen clearly reflects the chosen plant and intended placement
Naming feels meaningful rather than tacked on
Registration completes without confusion

userStories.title: Build care logs, final polish, full-playwright review, and Ralphthon demo readiness loop
userStories.description: As a demo operator, I want the product to survive end-to-end scrutiny so that the live Ralphthon run feels intentional, stable, and memorable.
userStories.goal: Finish the system with care logs, end-to-end validation, screenshot-based review, and a strict pass-until-satisfied loop across the full primary journey.
details: This story is not optional cleanup; it is the final demo hardening layer.
Do not mark the project complete until the full sequential journey has been reviewed cold with Playwright and screenshots.
deliverables: Full end-to-end Playwright review evidence set
acceptanceCriteria: Users can log watering, repotting, and nutrients with timestamps
The care log route is useful in both empty and populated states
All major routes have stable empty/error/loading states
A full end-to-end demo path has been executed and judged with screenshots
The app is only marked passed once the final review is genuinely satisfying

subtasks.title: Build care log route and action logging
details: Create a plant-specific care route with action buttons and a timeline or list of logged entries.
doneWhen: User can add multiple care actions and see them reflected immediately

subtasks.title: Implement empty, loading, and error states across major flows
details: Audit each primary route and add missing fallback states.
doneWhen: No primary route collapses into an unhandled blank or broken state

subtasks.title: Create a deterministic demo seed path
details: Make it easy to produce one reliable happy-path walkthrough from home to care log.
doneWhen: A reviewer can reproduce the main demo flow consistently

subtasks.title: Run full Playwright flow review with screenshots
details: Walk the full product sequentially and capture evidence at every major step.
doneWhen: Every major step in the final demo has screenshot evidence

subtasks.title: Perform cold Ralphthon readiness review and iterate until satisfied
details: Judge the product as if seeing it fresh in a competition setting and keep fixing issues until the wow moments and reliability both land.
doneWhen: No critical visual, flow, or stability issue remains and the demo feels compelling

evaluationSet.name: Command quality gates
passCriteria: Both commands exit successfully
No new warnings or build-time blockers remain unexplained
failActions: Do not continue to visual review until both commands pass

evaluationSet.name: Full end-to-end Playwright journey review
setup: Seed deterministic data only where unavoidable; prefer the real address path where stable
actions: Complete either the address path or the photo path
Observe the analysis loading hero moment
Use the placement board and trigger both good and bad placement responses
Log at least one care action
passCriteria: The full flow completes without blocker errors
Each major screen is screenshot-worthy
The three hero moments all land clearly
The app feels coherent from beginning to end rather than like disconnected demos
reviewRubric: Would a first-time viewer understand the product within 10 seconds?
Would the placement board create a memorable demo moment?
Does the full experience feel deliberate and premium?
Would you confidently show these screenshots to Ralphthon judges?
failActions: List every weak or broken moment in order of severity
Rerun the full journey rather than only the local screen
Keep looping until the full journey satisfies the review rubric

mustCheck: The full product is demo-worthy end to end
Every major screen survives a cold review
The final state is only accepted when genuinely satisfying

stopOnlyWhen: No critical visual or flow issue remains open
